articles.title: FOMC preview: No move expected as oil shock buries rate-cut hopes
articles.expand: The Iran war has done what months of sticky inflation data couldn’t: it has almost completely erased the market’s remaining expectations for Fed easing. The FOMC is overwhelmingly expected to leave rates unchanged tomorrow, with market pricing near 100% for no move. But the real story is further out the curve. articles.title: EUR/USD rebounds as Fed decision nears and US-Iran tensions cap Dollar downside
articles.expand: The Euro (EUR) trims a part of its intraday losses against the US Dollar (USD) on Tuesday as the Greenback loses momentum, with traders repositioning ahead of the Federal Reserve’s (Fed) interest rate decision due on Wednesday, while continuing to monitor developments in the Middle East.

articles.title: USD/JPY steadies after hawkish BoJ shift as Middle East tensions support Dollar
articles.expand: USD/JPY trades around 159.50 on Tuesday, up modestly by 0.07% on the day, after briefly falling below the 159.00 level following the Bank of Japan’s (BoJ) policy decision.

articles.title: EU replaces its top trade official following deal with Trump
articles.expand: Earlier today Politico reported (and now the FT confirms) that Sabine Weyand, the Director-General for Trade at the European Commission has been fired. articles.expand: United Overseas Bank’s Alvin Liew reports the Bank of Japan (BoJ) left its policy rate at 0.75% but signalled that the next move will be a hike as underlying inflation nears target and real rates stay very low.
